evals.prompt: I need to organize our 500+ sales documents into a proper content library. We have battle cards, case studies, one-pagers, and decks scattered across Google Drive. Our 30-person sales team can never find what they need. We recently started using Seismic and want to migrate everything there. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Content audit and migration plan with taxonomy, Seismic-specific setup guidance, and governance framework

assertions.name: content_taxonomy
assertions.description: Proposes organizing content by buyer journey stage, persona, and content type as primary dimensions

assertions.name: seismic_guidance
assertions.description: Includes Seismic-specific guidance on Content Profiles, Content Classes, and workspaces for organizing and governing content

assertions.name: audit_first
assertions.description: Recommends auditing and scoring existing content before migrating — don't move stale or unused content into the new system

evals.prompt: Create a battle card template for competitive deals. We compete against 3 main competitors and our reps often go into calls unprepared on competitive positioning. We need something they can quickly reference.
evals.expected_output: Battle card template with structure, length guidance, and maintenance plan

assertions.name: battle_card_structure
assertions.description: Provides battle card structure including overview, strengths/weaknesses, landmines (questions to plant), objection responses, and win stories

assertions.name: length_recommendation
assertions.description: Recommends keeping battle cards to 1-2 pages max so reps can scan them quickly before calls

assertions.name: update_cadence
assertions.description: Recommends monthly update cadence or updates triggered by competitor changes, with assigned ownership

evals.prompt: How do I measure which sales content actually helps close deals? We have a big content library but no idea what's working. Reps share content but we don't know if it makes a difference.
evals.expected_output: Content measurement framework distinguishing usage from effectiveness with specific tracking approach

assertions.name: content_deal_correlation
assertions.description: Recommends tracking which content is shared per opportunity to correlate content with deal outcomes

assertions.name: win_rate_metric
assertions.description: Includes win rate by content piece as a key metric — comparing win rates for deals where specific content was shared vs not shared

assertions.name: usage_vs_effectiveness
assertions.description: Distinguishes between usage metrics (downloads, views, shares) and effectiveness metrics (win correlation, deal velocity impact)
